title: Cordyline angusta
intro: Cordyline angusta, also known as the slender cabbage tree or New Zealand cabbage palm, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and anxiety, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cordyline angusta include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Cordyline angusta is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cordyline angusta is classified within the family Asparagaceae. It is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand. The genus Cordyline is part of the order Asparagales and the class Liliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics. Cordyline angusta is further grouped within the subfamily Lomandroideae.
